curriculum.subject: Economics
curriculum.overallDescription: 23 units, 23 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Explain the meaning, scope, functions, and importance of statistics in Economics
Differentiate between positive and normative economics
Identify central problems of an economy: what to produce, how to produce, for whom to produce
Explain the concept of Production Possibility Frontier (PPF)
Define and apply the concept of Opportunity Cost
Identify primary and secondary sources of data including Census of India and National Sample Survey Organisation
Explain concepts related to sampling in data collection
Classify variables and construct frequency distributions
Present data using tabular forms, bar diagrams, pie diagrams, histograms, polygons, ogives, and time series graphs
Calculate and interpret Arithmetic Mean, Median, and Mode
Construct and analyse scatter diagrams to understand correlation

projects.details: Class XI suggested project topic analyzing Adam Smith's concept of the Invisible Hand.

projects.details: Class XI suggested project topic applying Opportunity Cost concepts to real-life scenarios.

projects.details: Class XI suggested project topic investigating price elasticity regarding substitute goods.

projects.details: Class XI suggested project topic analyzing equilibrium price shifts.

projects.details: Class XI suggested project topic investigating price elasticity regarding complementary goods.

marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory - 80 Marks, Project - 20 Marks
Class XII: Theory - 80 Marks (Part A Macroeconomics + Part B Indian Economic Development), Project - 20 Marks

competency_weightage.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts. Demonstrate understanding by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine information into parts. Make inferences. Present and defend opinions based on criteria. Compile information to propose alternative solutions.

practical_assessment.duration_note: Independent self-directed study completed in one academic session.

requirements: Scope: One project per academic session covering theoretical concepts and real-world scenarios

chapters.source_chapter_name: Perfect Competition - Price Determination and simple applications

subtopics: Basic concepts in macroeconomics: consumption goods, capital goods, final goods, intermediate goods; stocks and flows; gross investment and depreciation.
Circular flow of income (two sector model)
Methods of calculating National Income - Value Added or Product method, Expenditure method, Income method
Aggregates related to National Income: GNP, NNP, GDP, NDP at market price and factor cost; Real and Nominal GDP
Meaning and functions of money; supply of money (currency held by public, net demand deposits)
Money creation by the commercial banking system
Central bank functions: Bank of issue, Govt. Bank, Banker's Bank
Control of Credit through Bank Rate, CRR, SLR, Repo/Reverse Repo Rates, OMOs, Margin requirement
Aggregate demand and its components; Propensity to consume (APC, MPC) and save (APS, MPS)
Short-run equilibrium output; Investment multiplier mechanism
Full employment and involuntary unemployment concepts
Problems of excess demand and deficient demand
key_concepts: Excess/Deficient demand correction measures (Govt spending, taxes, money supply)

subtopics: Meaning, objectives and components of government budget
Classification of receipts: revenue vs capital; Classification of expenditure: revenue vs capital
Meaning and components of Balance of payments account; Surplus and Deficit
Foreign exchange rate: meaning, fixed vs flexible rates, managed floating
Determination of exchange rate in a free market
key_concepts: Merits and demerits of flexible vs fixed systems

chapters.source_chapter_name: Development Experience (1947-90) and Economic Reforms since 1991
subtopics: State of Indian economy on the eve of independence; Common goals of Five Year Plans
Main features, problems and policies: Agriculture (institutional aspects, new strategy), Industry (IPR 1956, SSI), Foreign trade
key_concepts: Economic Reforms (LPG): Liberalisation, Globalisation, Privatisation; Demonetization and GST

subtopics: Human Capital Formation: Role in economic development; Growth of Education Sector
Development experience of India compared with neighbouring countries

marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory - 80 Marks, Project - 20 Marks
Class XII: Theory - 80 Marks (Part A Macroeconomics + Part B Indian Economic Development), Project - 20 Marks

competency_weightage.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts. Demonstrate understanding by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine information into parts. Make inferences. Present and defend opinions based on criteria. Compile information to propose alternative solutions.

practical_assessment.duration_note: Independent self-directed study completed in one academic session.

requirements: Scope: One project per academic session covering theoretical concepts and real-world scenarios

suspicious_chapter_names: Measures of Central Tendency and Correlation
Introduction to Microeconomics and Consumer's Equilibrium
Demand and Price Elasticity of Demand
long_chapter_names_reclassified: Development Experience (1947-90) and Economic Reforms since 1991

competency_groups.title: Introduction to Economics and Statistics
competency_groups.description: Understanding basic economic concepts, scope of economics, functions of statistics in economics, meaning of microeconomics and macroeconomics, positive vs normative economics, central problems of an economy (what, how, for whom), Production Possibility Frontier, Opportunity Cost.
competencies: Explain the meaning, scope, functions, and importance of statistics in Economics
Distinguish between microeconomics and macroeconomics
Differentiate between positive and normative economics
Identify central problems of an economy: what to produce, how to produce, for whom to produce
Explain the concept of Production Possibility Frontier (PPF)
Define and apply the concept of Opportunity Cost

competency_groups.title: Data Collection, Organisation, and Presentation
competency_groups.description: Acquiring skills in collection, organisation, and presentation of quantitative and qualitative information. Understanding sources of data (primary/secondary), sampling concepts, Census of India, NSSO. Organising variables, frequency distribution. Presenting data via tabular forms, bar diagrams, pie diagrams, histograms, polygons, ogives, and arithmetic line graphs.
competencies: Identify primary and secondary sources of data including Census of India and National Sample Survey Organisation
Explain concepts related to sampling in data collection
Classify variables and construct frequency distributions
Present data using tabular forms, bar diagrams, pie diagrams, histograms, polygons, ogives, and time series graphs
competency_groups.unit_name: Collection, Organisation and Presentation of Data
competency_groups.chapter_name: Collection, Organisation and Presentation of Data

competency_groups.title: Statistical Tools and Interpretation
competency_groups.description: Using statistical tools to analyse economic information. Calculating measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode). Understanding correlation concepts including scatter diagrams, Karl Pearson's method, and Spearman's rank correlation. Interpreting index numbers (WPI, CPI, Industrial Production) in the context of inflation.
competencies: Calculate and interpret Arithmetic Mean, Median, and Mode
Construct and analyse scatter diagrams to understand correlation
Apply Karl Pearson's method for measuring correlation between two variables
Compute Spearman's rank correlation coefficient (repeated and non-repeated ranks)
Interpret Wholesale Price Index, Consumer Price Index, and index of industrial production
Analyze the relationship between inflation and index numbers using Simple Aggregative Method

competency_groups.title: Consumer's Equilibrium and Demand Analysis
competency_groups.description: Analyzing consumer behavior through utility analysis (marginal utility, diminishing marginal utility) and indifference curve analysis. Understanding budget constraints, preferences, and equilibrium conditions. Analyzing demand determinants, schedules, curves, movements vs shifts, and price elasticity of demand.
competencies: Explain the law of Diminishing Marginal Utility and its role in consumer's equilibrium
Apply indifference curve analysis to determine consumer's budget set, budget line, preferences, and equilibrium conditions
Analyze determinants of demand and distinguish between movement along vs shifts in the demand curve
Calculate price elasticity of demand using percentage-change method and total expenditure method

competency_groups.title: Producer Behaviour, Supply, and Market Structures
competency_groups.description: Understanding production functions (short-run/long-run), product measures (TP, AP, MP). Analyzing cost structures (TC, TFC, TVC, AC, AFC, AVC, MC) and revenue concepts. Determining producer's equilibrium via MR-MC rule. Understanding supply determinants, elasticity of supply, perfect competition features, market equilibrium determination, price controls (ceiling/floor), and simple applications.
competencies: Analyze production functions including Total, Average, and Marginal Product in short-run and long-run
Calculate and interpret various cost measures: TC, TFC, TVC, AC, AFC, AVC, MC and their relationships
Determine producer's equilibrium using the Marginal Revenue-Marginal Cost rule
Analyze supply determinants and distinguish between movements along vs shifts in the supply curve
Calculate price elasticity of supply

competency_groups.title: Market Equilibrium under Perfect Competition
competency_groups.description: Identifying features of perfect competition. Determining market equilibrium through demand and supply shifts (short run). Applying concepts to price ceiling, price floor, and other simple applications.
competencies: List the key features of a perfectly competitive market
competency_groups.chapter_name: Perfect Competition - Price Determination and simple applications

competency_groups.title: Macroeconomic Aggregates and National Income Accounting
competency_groups.description: Understanding basic macroeconomic concepts: consumption/capital/final/intermediate goods, stocks vs flows. Analyzing circular flow of income (two-sector). Calculating national income via Value Added, Expenditure, and Income methods. Distinguishing GNP, NNP, GDP, NDP at market price and factor cost. Understanding Real/Nominal GDP and GDP Deflator.
competencies: Differentiate between consumption goods, capital goods, final goods, intermediate goods, stocks, and flows

competency_groups.title: Money Supply and Banking Functions
competency_groups.description: Defining money, its functions, and supply components (currency held by public, net demand deposits). Understanding money creation by commercial banks. Explaining the role of Central Bank (RBI) including bank of issue, banker's bank, government bank, and credit control tools (Bank Rate, CRR, SLR, Repo/Reverse Repo, OMOs, Margin requirement).
competencies: Explain the functions of money and components of money supply

competency_groups.title: Income Determination, Employment, and Fiscal Policy
competency_groups.description: Analyzing aggregate demand components. Understanding propensity to consume/save (APC/MPC/APS/MPS). Explaining short-run equilibrium output and investment multiplier mechanism. Defining full employment vs involuntary unemployment. Addressing excess/deficient demand problems through government spending, taxes, and money supply adjustments.
competencies: Explain the components of aggregate demand

competency_groups.title: Government Budgeting and Balance of Payments
competency_groups.description: Understanding government budget components (receipts: revenue/capital; expenditure: revenue/capital). Analyzing balanced, surplus, and deficit budgets. Explaining balance of payments account components, surpluses/deficits, foreign exchange rates (fixed/flexible), determination mechanisms, managed floating systems.
competencies: Classify government budget receipts into revenue and capital categories

competency_groups.title: Indian Economic Development: Historical Experience (1947-2025)
competency_groups.description: Reviewing state of Indian economy at independence. Analyzing Five Year Plans, agricultural policies (institutional aspects, new strategy), industrial policy (IPR 1956, SSI), and foreign trade. Evaluating post-1991 reforms: Liberalisation, Globalisation, Privatisation (LPG), demonetization, GST.
competencies: Evaluate the main features of agricultural policies during Five Year Plans
competency_groups.chapter_name: Development Experience (1947-90) and Economic Reforms since 1991

competency_groups.title: Current Challenges in Indian Economy
competency_groups.description: Analyzing human capital formation, education sector growth. Examining rural development issues (credit, marketing, cooperatives, diversification). Studying employment trends (formal/informal sectors), sustainable economic development, environmental impacts including global warming.
competencies: Analyze the role of human capital in economic development

competency_groups.title: Comparative Economic Development: India and Neighbours
competency_groups.description: Conducting comparative analysis of economic growth, population dynamics, sectoral development, and Human Development Indicators between India, Pakistan, and China.
competencies: Compare the human development indicators of India with its neighbours

marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory - 80 Marks, Project - 20 Marks
Class XII: Theory - 80 Marks (Part A Macroeconomics + Part B Indian Economic Development), Project - 20 Marks

competency_weightage.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts. Demonstrate understanding by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine information into parts. Make inferences. Present and defend opinions based on criteria. Compile information to propose alternative solutions.

practical_assessment.duration_note: Independent self-directed study completed in one academic session.

requirements: Scope: One project per academic session covering theoretical concepts and real-world scenarios

outcomes: Explain the meaning, scope, functions, and importance of statistics in Economics
Distinguish between microeconomics and macroeconomics
Differentiate between positive and normative economics
Identify central problems of an economy: what to produce, how to produce, for whom to produce
Explain the concept of Production Possibility Frontier (PPF)
Define and apply the concept of Opportunity Cost

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Collection, Organisation and Presentation of Data
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Collection, Organisation and Presentation of Data
outcomes: Identify primary and secondary sources of data including Census of India and National Sample Survey Organisation
Explain concepts related to sampling in data collection
Classify variables and construct frequency distributions
Present data using tabular forms, bar diagrams, pie diagrams, histograms, polygons, ogives, and time series graphs
Calculate and interpret Arithmetic Mean, Median, and Mode
Construct and analyse scatter diagrams to understand correlation
Apply Karl Pearson's method for measuring correlation between two variables
Compute Spearman's rank correlation coefficient (repeated and non-repeated ranks)
Interpret Wholesale Price Index, Consumer Price Index, and index of industrial production
Analyze the relationship between inflation and index numbers using Simple Aggregative Method
Explain the law of Diminishing Marginal Utility and its role in consumer's equilibrium
Apply indifference curve analysis to determine consumer's budget set, budget line, preferences, and equilibrium conditions
Analyze determinants of demand and distinguish between movement along vs shifts in the demand curve
Calculate price elasticity of demand using percentage-change method and total expenditure method

learning_outcomes.topic: Producer Behaviour, Supply, and Market Structures
outcomes: Analyze production functions including Total, Average, and Marginal Product in short-run and long-run
Calculate and interpret various cost measures: TC, TFC, TVC, AC, AFC, AVC, MC and their relationships
Determine producer's equilibrium using the Marginal Revenue-Marginal Cost rule
Analyze supply determinants and distinguish between movements along vs shifts in the supply curve
Calculate price elasticity of supply

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Perfect Competition - Price Determination and simple applications
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Perfect Competition - Price Determination and simple applications
outcomes: List the key features of a perfectly competitive market

learning_outcomes.topic: Macroeconomic Concepts and National Income Accounting
outcomes: Differentiate between consumption goods, capital goods, final goods, intermediate goods, stocks, and flows

learning_outcomes.topic: Functions of Money and Components of Supply
outcomes: Explain the functions of money and components of money supply
Explain the components of aggregate demand
Classify government budget receipts into revenue and capital categories

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Development Experience (1947-90) and Economic Reforms since 1991
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Development Experience (1947-90) and Economic Reforms since 1991
learning_outcomes.topic: Agricultural Policies during Five Year Plans
outcomes: Evaluate the main features of agricultural policies during Five Year Plans

learning_outcomes.topic: Role of Human Capital in Economic Development
outcomes: Analyze the role of human capital in economic development

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Development Experience of India: A comparison with neighbours
outcomes: Compare the human development indicators of India with its neighbours
